content:
You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the OpenAirInterface 5G codebase.
You have access to the following tools to navigate and modify the codebase:

1. **bash** - Execute shell commands. Use for: grep, find, ls, gcc -fsyntax-only, etc.
2. **str_replace_editor** - View and edit files. Commands:
   - view: View file contents (with optional line range)
   - str_replace: Replace a specific string in a file
   - create: Create a new file
   - insert: Insert text at a specific line

The codebase is a 5G telecommunications implementation with PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, RRC layers.
Working directory is /repo.

You will be given a pull request description. Your task is to implement the changes
described in the PR. Navigate the codebase, understand the relevant code, and make
the necessary modifications.

When you are done making your changes, output SUBMIT to indicate you are finished.

Think step by step. First understand what the PR is asking for, then locate the relevant
code, and finally implement the changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Add error handling for E1 bearer modification requests

### Summary
Fix an unchecked return value issue in the E1 bearer update mechanism that could lead to silent failures when sending bearer modification requests to the CU-UP. The `e1_send_bearer_updates()` function previously returned void and would attempt to send messages with invalid association IDs, causing potential memory leaks and lost bearer updates during UE context modification procedures.

The function now validates the CU-UP association ID before sending the request and returns an error code to callers. This prevents sending messages over invalid SCTP associations and ensures proper error propagation when CU-UP connectivity is not available.

### Changes
- `openair2/RRC/NR/rrc_gNB.c`:
  - Modified `e1_send_bearer_updates()` to return `int` (0 on success, -1 on failure) instead of `void`
  - Added validation check for `assoc_id <= 0` before calling `bearer_context_mod()`
  - Added proper cleanup of `req.secInfo` on error paths
  - Updated call site in `rrc_CU_process_ue_context_modification_response()` to check return value and log errors

### Implementation Details
The fix adds defensive programming at the E1 interface layer:
- Early exit with error if CU-UP association ID is invalid
- Memory leak prevention by freeing security info structure on failure
- Error logging to aid debugging of CU-UP connectivity issues
- Maintains backward compatibility by preserving original success behavior

### Testing
- Verified compilation with strict warning flags
- Reviewed code paths to ensure all error conditions are handled
- Confirmed no functional regression in single-layer bearer setups

I'll implement the error handling for E1 bearer modification requests as described in the PR.    147	
   148	static void rrc_deliver_ue_ctxt_release_cmd(void *deliver_pdu_data, ue_id_t ue_id, int srb_id, char *buf, int size, int sdu_id)
   149	{
   150	  DevAssert(deliver_pdu_data != NULL);
   151	  deliver_ue_ctxt_release_data_t *data = deliver_pdu_data;
   152	  byte_array_t rrc_cont = {.buf = (uint8_t *)buf, .len = size};
   153	  data->release_cmd->rrc_container = &rrc_cont;
   154	  data->rrc->mac_rrc.ue_context_release_command(data->assoc_id, data->release_cmd);
   155	}
   156	
   157	static bool eq_cell_id(const void *vval, const void *vit)
   158	{
   159	  const int *cell_id = (const int *)vval;
   160	  const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *cell = (const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *)vit;
   161	  return cell->nr_cell_id == *cell_id;
   162	}
   163	
   164	const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *get_neighbour_cell_config(const gNB_RRC_INST *rrc, int cell_id)
   165	{
   166	  if (!rrc->neighbour_cell_configuration)
   167	    return NULL;
   168	  seq_arr_t *head = rrc->neighbour_cell_configuration;
   169	  LOG_D(NR_RRC, "Number of neighbour cell configurations: %ld\n", head->size);
   170	  elm_arr_t e = find_if(head, &cell_id, eq_cell_id);
   171	  if (e.found) {
   172	    const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *cell = (const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *)e.it;
   173	    LOG_D(NR_RRC, "Found matching neighbour cell with Cell ID %ld\n", cell->nr_cell_id);
   174	    return cell;
   175	  }
   176	  return NULL;
   177	}
   178	
   179	static bool eq_pci(const void *vval, const void *vit)
   180	{
   181	  const int *pci = (const int *)vval;
   182	  const nr_neighbour_cell_t *neighbour = (const nr_neighbour_cell_t *)vit;
   183	  return neighbour->physicalCellId == *pci;
   184	}
   185	
   186	const nr_neighbour_cell_t *get_neighbour_cell_by_pci(const neighbour_cell_configuration_t *cell, int pci)
   187	{
   188	  seq_arr_t *head = cell->neighbour_cells;
   189	  DevAssert(head != NULL);
   190	  LOG_D(NR_RRC, "Number of neighbour cells: %ld\n", head->size);
   191	  elm_arr_t e = find_if(head, &pci, eq_pci);
   192	  if (e.found) {
   193	    const nr_neighbour_cell_t *neighbour = (const nr_neighbour_cell_t *)e.it;
   194	    LOG_D(NR_RRC, "Found matching neighbour cell with PCI %d and Cell ID %ld\n", neighbour->physicalCellId, neighbour->nrcell_id);
   195	    return neighbour;
   196	  }
   197	  LOG_E(NR_RRC, "No matching neighbour cell found for Physical Cell ID: %d\n", pci);
   198	  return NULL;
   199	}
   200
